content:
A practical guide to automating your workflows using OpenAI’s new desktop agent and the Soul model.

4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by ilgmyzin on UnsplashThe tech industry moves aggressively fast. As a software engineer, trying to build a solid foundation, keeping up with every new AI release can feel completely exhausting. Most updates give us slightly better text generation or faster response times. But OpenAI just rolled out something that fundamentally changes how we interact with our machines.
They introduced ChatGPT Work. This is a massive shift. The tool has evolved from a simple chatbot that answers your questions into a background agent that executes tasks on its own. After spending time diving into the official documentation and the latest developer breakdowns, I want to share exactly what this means for us. This is an advanced look at how you can leverage these new systems to automate the tedious parts of your engineering job.
The New Intelligence Engine
Before we look at the shiny new features, we have to understand the engines powering them. The ChatGPT 5.6 update brings three brand new models designed specifically for agentic workflows. They are named Soul, Terra, and Luna.
